description: Hark llamadart quantization benchmark — matrix of (model, quant) combinations to test. Uses ONLY first-party / top-tier GGUF publishers: ggml-org (official llama.cpp team) for EmbeddingGemma, Qwen (model author) for Qwen3. Neither publishes Q4_K_M or Q5_K_M for these specific models, so we test Q8_0 only. If Q8_0 passes the quality gate with room to spare, we ship it. If we later want a smaller variant for size reasons, that becomes an explicit trust decision about community quantizers — not something to default into.
trust_tier_note: Current main branch ships onnx-community/embeddinggemma-300m-ONNX (model_q4.onnx) and litert-community/Qwen3-0.6B (Qwen3-0.6B.litertlm). These are the format-specific first-party orgs for ONNX and LiteRT-LM respectively. ggml-org and Qwen are the equivalent first-party tier for GGUF.

quants.publisher_note: ggml-org is the official llama.cpp team — the GGUF format's inventors and the GGUF equivalent of onnx-community for ONNX.
Published by the Qwen team themselves — directly from Alibaba's model authors, same source as the litert-community Qwen3-0.6B.litertlm we currently ship.

embedder_only_migration.when: If Qwen3 Q8_0 fails the slot-filling quality gate, abandon the slot-filling migration and keep flutter_gemma for Qwen3. Only migrate the embedder.
embedder_only_migration.implication: Hark keeps two plugins (llamadart for embeddings, flutter_gemma for slot-fill) but still gets mmap + warm engine + isolate safety for the bigger model (the embedder).

community_quant_escalation.when: If we later need a smaller quant than Q8_0 for file-size reasons, the fallback is a trusted community quantizer (unsloth, bartowski, mradermacher) — but this is an EXPLICIT trust decision, not a default. Any switch away from ggml-org / Qwen must be justified by measured size or perf wins that cannot be ignored.
